name: Darul Qasim
mission: TO FACILITATE THE LEARNING AND IMPARTING OF KNOKLEDGE DERIVED FROM PROPHETHOOD OF MUHAMMAD , ARABIC LANGUAGE AND RELIGIOUS EDUCATION
theoryOfChange: By re-presenting the Islamic intellectual tradition with clarity and academic rigor, the organization aims to produce scholars capable of navigating modern challenges and inspiring conviction in the Truth.
scoreSummary: Darul Qasim shows moderate alignment and moderate impact, with zakat compliance (minor risk deductions applied).

keyConcerns.headline: Zakat-collecting organization holds 29 months of reserves

dimension_explanations.alignment: With an alignment score of 27/50, the charity offers a medium fit for donors interested in global education and Islamic scholarship [6]. It provides clear avenues for zakat contributions to support its mission of imparting knowledge derived from the Prophethood of Muhammad [3][7].
dimension_explanations.impact: The organization focuses on capacity building through advanced religious programs such as the Iftā Program and Takhaṣṣuṣ fī al-Tafsīr [4][5]. While it maintains a healthy 29.3 months of working capital, its impact score of 25/50 reflects a program expense ratio of 51.9%, indicating that just over half of its $2,696,418 in total revenue is spent directly on its primary educational programs [1][2].

baseline_narrative.headline: Darul Qasim is an Islamic institution of higher learning dedicated to preserving and imparting traditional knowledge derived from the Prophetic tradition.
strengths: High fundraising efficiency with only $0.02 spent to raise every $1
Strong liquidity position with 29.3 months of working capital available
Clear zakat eligibility and distribution policy for eligible students
baseline_narrative.summary: Based in Illinois, Darul Qasim facilitates the study of the Arabic language and religious education through specialized academic tracks [1][4]. The organization is recognized as zakat-eligible, allowing donors to support its educational mission through various giving methods [3][7].

strengths.detail: The organization demonstrates high fiscal discipline in its development efforts, spending only <cite id="2">$0.02 to raise every $1</cite> in contributions.
Darul Qasim maintains a significant safety net with <cite id="2">29.3 months of working capital</cite> held in working capital, ensuring long-term institutional stability.
The institution provides unique <cite id="4">advanced programs in Islamic Law and Theology</cite> that are rarely available at this depth in the United States.

strengths_deep_dive: Darul Qasim's primary strength lies in its institutional stability and specialized niche within the American Muslim educational landscape. The organization has successfully scaled its operations, reaching <cite id="1">$3,290,091 in annual revenue</cite> while maintaining <cite id="2">100% independent board governance</cite>. This governance structure supports a <cite id="6">documented theory of change</cite> centered on capacity building through traditional epistemology. Furthermore, the institution's ability to generate <cite id="1">$835,237 in program service revenue</cite> suggests a sustainable model that does not rely solely on donations, but also on the value of its educational offerings.
rich_narrative.summary: Darul Qasim is an Illinois-based institution of higher learning dedicated to <cite id="1">facilitating the learning and imparting of knowledge</cite> derived from the Prophetic tradition. The organization operates as a specialized academic center, offering advanced tracks such as the <cite id="4">Iftā Program and Takhaṣṣuṣ fī al-Tafsīr</cite>, which focus on Islamic law and theology. Financially, the institution reported <cite id="1">total revenue of $3,290,091</cite> in the 2023 tax year, reflecting a significant <cite id="1">3-year revenue CAGR of 23.4%</cite>. While the organization maintains a <cite id="2">strong liquidity position with 29.3 months of working capital</cite>, its <cite id="1">program expense ratio stands at 51.9%</cite>, which is lower than the <cite id="1">peer program ratio median of 89.6%</cite> for global education charities. For Muslim donors, Darul Qasim provides a <cite id="3">verified zakat eligibility policy</cite>, specifically directing funds toward <cite id="3">eligible students and educational initiatives</cite> that support the preservation of traditional Islamic sciences.
